dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. The village is falling apart. People are sick, scared. Tell me what you've heard.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: More than I'd like. Sarah's still down in the laboratory — she's been working on something. A cure, if you believe the whispers.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Where would I find a way in? The lab's locked, and there's no power running to it.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: The key was buried with the old caretaker. Graveyard. As for power — George keeps the grid alive. He'd know what the lab needs.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And Sarah herself?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Last I heard, she wasn't well. If you're going down there, be ready for what you find. And carry something to fix her with.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. I need to get power back to the laboratory.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Line's intact. Just needs a fuse. There's one inside the lab itself — old stock.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll have to unlock the place first. Lab key's at the graveyard.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Then go dig. Bring the fuse back here. I'll route the power when you do.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Stop right there. State your business.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Looking for a cure sample. Sarah's down in the lab, infected. I can fix this if I get it to her.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Sample's on the bench. Take it. If it actually works, the village has a chance.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It will. Hold this position.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Wasn't planning on going anywhere. Move.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. Stay with me. I have the sample.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: You're... late. But not too late, I think. The synthesizer is functional now — I can feel the power humming.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Walk me through it.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Load the sample. Let the machine do the rest. Then... use it on me. Quickly. My cognition is degrading.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Hold on. I'm doing it now.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. Something on this island isn't natural. The outbreak, the way people are disappearing into the outpost — someone's orchestrating it.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Took you long enough to ask. People talk in here. They think I don't listen. I do.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then tell me. Who's pulling the strings?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Viktor holds the gun, but he's not the brain. The Doctor — he's been moving supplies through the outpost for months. Chemicals. Cages. Things no clinic orders.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The Doctor. Where is he now?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Deep in the outpost. Past Viktor. You won't get through that door without the key — and the only one I know of is in the cave. Hidden where Viktor's men don't go.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And George at the powerstation — does he know anything?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: He knows where the lines run. The outpost draws more power than it should. Ask him. He'll tell you straight.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. The outpost — what's it drawing from the grid?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Too much. Three times what a barracks needs. Refrigeration loads. Centrifuges. Something cold and constant.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: A lab.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: That'd fit. Whoever runs it knows what they're doing. Clean wiring. Professional.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm going in. Anything I should know?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: If you need to shut him down, kill the line from here. But pull the wrong breaker and you're dark too. Get me proof he's the problem and I'll handle the grid.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: You walked in here on your own. That's either courage or stupidity. I haven't decided which.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Neither. I came to talk. I know you're not the one running this. The Doctor is.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: Careful. That kind of talk gets people buried.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: He's using you, Viktor. Your men, your name, your bullets — to cover an experiment he runs in a back room. When this island burns, he walks away with the data and you take the blame.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: ...Keep talking.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Power draws three times what a garrison needs. Refrigeration. Centrifuges. He's not protecting anyone. He's farming them.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: I don't owe him anything. Go. See for yourself. If you're right, the back room is yours. If you're wrong, my men finish you on the way out.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Ah. The subject ambulates into the laboratory of its own accord. Fascinating.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You started this. The outbreak. All of it.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Started is a strong word. I introduced a variable. The population responded. The data has been... extraordinary.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: People are dying.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: People die regardless. What I have learned will outlast every name on this island, including yours. Including mine.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then give me what I need to fix it. The original sample. The fuse to power Sarah's synthesizer. The lab key. Or Viktor finds out exactly who he's been bleeding for.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: A crude leverage. Effective, though. Take them. The work continues with or without these instruments. I have already transmitted the relevant findings.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: We'll see about that.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. I found him. The Doctor. He's the one who engineered the outbreak.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I suspected as much. The pathogen's structure is too clean to be natural. Someone designed it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: He admitted it. Said he's already transmitted his findings.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then we deny him the only thing he cares about — being the one who finishes it. If we produce a working cure first, his legacy is a footnote.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: What do you need?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: A viable sample, and the synthesizer at full power. The cure work is mine — but we'll need help to keep this place standing while I work. Talk to Marcus at the hospital. He'll know how to harden the lab.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. I'll start moving pieces.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah. I think it's time. We need to get off this island.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I had reached the same conclusion. The research is at a stopping point — and Viktor will not let us continue much longer.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary's boat at the dock. If we can get it running, that's our way out.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then that is the path. You'll need wood and a toolkit — and Mary's cooperation. I'll secure what's left of my work here and meet you at the dock when you call.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anyone else worth bringing?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Anne. George. Linda, if she'll come. Marcus will hold his post until the end — but offer it to him. He deserves the choice.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary. What does it take to put your boat back in the water?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Hull's cracked along the keel. I need solid wood and a toolkit. Get me that and I'll have her seaworthy by sundown.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I've got a toolkit. Wood's lying around the village — I'll grab a few planks.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then quit talking and start hauling. Anyone who helps gets a seat when she sails. That's the deal.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. Stand by — I'll be back.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. Mary's fixing the boat. We're leaving the island. Tonight, if we can manage it.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: I wondered when somebody would say that out loud. Who's going?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You, if you want a seat. Sarah. George, if I can pry him off that grid. Linda. Marcus is his own decision.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: I'll lock up the store and be at the dock. Don't forget Linda — she won't ask, but she'll come if you offer.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'll head there next.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: And watch the village roads. Viktor's people have been moving more than usual.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Linda. There's a boat at the dock. Mary's getting it running. We're going.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Off the island.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Off the island. You should come.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: I've thought about it. Land's gone quiet in a way I don't like. I'll come.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Bring whatever food you can carry. We'll need it.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Already packed. Been packed a while.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. We've got a boat. Mary's repairing it now. There's a seat for you.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Boat. Hm.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The grid won't matter if Viktor takes the village. You know that.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: I do. I'll shut her down clean. Then I'll come.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Meet us at the dock. Don't be late.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: I'm never late.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Marcus. Mary's boat is going out tonight. You've got a seat if you want it.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Appreciated. Not taking it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Why?

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Someone has to hold the line for anyone who comes after. Hospital's defensible. I'll fortify it and dig in.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I brought wood. Use it.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Drop it. I'll do the rest. Go — and don't look back from that water.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Got the wood. Toolkit too. Let's do this.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: About time. Hand me the planks — I'll work the patch while you brace the hull.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Others are coming. Anne, Sarah, George, Linda.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Good. More hands at the oars if the engine quits. Now — let's get this old girl sailing.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: She'll hold. Engine's coughing but she'll hold.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then cast off. The others are right behind us.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Untie the bow line. We sail with the tide.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Goodbye, island.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Don't say goodbye. Say good riddance. Now sit down and hold on — open water from here.
